# Task File Format Guide

Task files are filled in two phases — you are not required to complete all fields at once.
The following are complete examples for each phase.

---

## Phase 1: At Creation (draft status, generated by AI from raw requirements)

```markdown
---
id: TASK-AUTH-0001
title: "Implement global Axios request/response interceptors"
status: draft
priority: high            # high | medium | low
services: []              # repos involved, fill in after development, can be multiple
assignee: ""              # responsible party's GitHub username
created: 2026-06-22
updated: 2026-06-22
depends_on: []            # prerequisite task IDs, e.g. [TASK-AUTH-0001]
wiki_refs: []             # fill in after Spec is archived to .wiki/
code_files: []            # fill in by developer after development
test_refs: []             # fill in by developer after development
---

## Raw Requirements

The request layer needs a unified Token injection mechanism, unified error response handling,
and static fallback data when an API call fails — to prevent blank page crashes.

## Spec

> ⚠️ Temporary section: generated by AI from raw requirements.
> After human review, extract acceptance criteria into the [Acceptance Criteria] section below
> and set status to `spec-ready`.
> Before merging the PR, archive this section to the corresponding file in `.wiki/features/`,
> fill in `wiki_refs`

### Background

(Describe the current problem or situation, and explain why this needs to be done)

### Goals

(List the specific goals this task should achieve, one per line)

### Out of Scope

(Explicitly list what is excluded to prevent scope creep)

### Technical Design

(API design, data flow, key implementation ideas; may include code snippets, SQL, type definitions)

### Affected Files

| File Path | Change Description |
|-----------|-------------------|
| (to be filled) | (add / modify / delete) |

## Acceptance Criteria

> Extract the core acceptance items from [Spec], fill in after human review

(To be filled after Spec is generated)

## Status Change History

| Time | Previous Status | New Status | Actor | Notes |
|------|-----------------|------------|-------|-------|
| 2026-06-22 | — | draft | AI | Raw requirements converted to structured TASK |
```

---

## Phase 2: After Development (dev-done status, developer fills in all remaining fields)

```markdown
---
id: TASK-AUTH-0001
title: "Implement global Axios request/response interceptors"
status: dev-done
priority: high
services:
  - frontend
  - bff
assignee: "dev-username"
created: 2026-06-22
updated: 2026-06-23
depends_on: []
wiki_refs:
  - .wiki/features/auth.md
code_files:
  - points-mall-frontend/src/lib/axios/interceptors.ts
  - points-mall-frontend/src/lib/axios/index.ts
  - points-mall-bff/src/auth/auth.controller.ts
test_refs:
  - points-mall-frontend/src/lib/axios/__tests__/interceptors.test.ts
  - .tests/api/bff/auth/login.bru
  - .tests/e2e/auth.spec.ts
---

## Raw Requirements

The request layer needs a unified Token injection mechanism, unified error response handling,
and static fallback data when an API call fails — to prevent blank page crashes.

## Spec

> ⚠️ Temporary section: archive to `.wiki/features/auth.md` before merging the PR,
> fill in `wiki_refs`, and **delete this section**.

### Background

All API requests in the project go through Axios, but there is currently no unified interceptor
layer. Token injection and error handling logic are scattered across individual pages, and the
page crashes with a blank screen when an API call fails.

### Goals

1. Request interceptor: unified injection of Authorization Bearer Token and baseURL
2. Response interceptor: unified handling of 401 (silent token refresh) / 403 (redirect to permissions page)
3. Fallback: return static fallback data on API failure so the page does not crash

### Out of Scope

- Do not modify existing business request logic in individual pages
- Do not implement the actual authentication endpoint for Token refresh

### Technical Design

Create an interceptor module under `points-mall-frontend/src/lib/axios/`:
- `interceptors.ts`: request/response interceptor logic
- `index.ts`: Axios instance creation + interceptor registration + fallback entry point
- `fallback/`: static JSON files organized by API path

Token is read from the Zustand auth store; fallback data is indexed by `url` path key.

### Affected Files

| File Path | Change Description |
|-----------|-------------------|
| `points-mall-frontend/src/lib/axios/interceptors.ts` | New file |
| `points-mall-frontend/src/lib/axios/index.ts` | New file |
| `points-mall-bff/src/auth/auth.controller.ts` | Modified: added 401/403 response format |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [x] Request interceptor: automatically injects Authorization Bearer Token
- [x] Request interceptor: unified baseURL prefix appended
- [x] Response interceptor: unified handling of 401 (silent refresh) / 403 (redirect to permissions page)
- [x] Fallback: returns static fallback data on API error — page does not crash

## Status Change History

| Time | Previous Status | New Status | Actor | Notes |
|------|-----------------|------------|-------|-------|
| 2026-06-22 | — | draft | AI | Raw requirements converted to structured TASK |
| 2026-06-22 | draft | spec-pending | Human | TASK content confirmed |
| 2026-06-22 | spec-pending | spec-ready | Human | Acceptance criteria filled in and review passed |
| 2026-06-23 | spec-ready | in-dev | dev-username | Development started, branch feat/TASK-AUTH-0001 |
| 2026-06-23 | in-dev | dev-done | dev-username | PR #12 opened |
```
